tasks.name: Create a new discussion in 'Web Development' with specific title and tags and subscribe to it
tasks.description: Start a new discussion thread in the 'Web Development' category titled 'Best resources to learn responsive design?', add exactly the tags 'CSS' and 'Responsive Design', write a body of at least 3 sentences, and subscribe to receive notifications for that thread.
steps: Navigate to the forum homepage by entering the site URL in the browser and pressing Enter.
Click the 'Log In' button in the top-right corner of the header.
In the login form, type 'sample_user' into the username field and 'Password123' into the password field, then click the 'Log In' submit button.
From the main navigation, click on the 'Categories' link and then select the 'Web Development' category from the list.
On the 'Web Development' category page, click the 'New Discussion' or 'Create Topic' button near the top of the thread list.
In the title field of the new discussion form, type 'Best resources to learn responsive design?'.
In the main content/body text area, type at least three complete sentences describing your current experience with responsive design and what kind of resources you are looking for.
In the tags input field, type 'CSS' and confirm it as a tag, then type 'Responsive Design' and confirm it as a second tag so there are exactly two tags attached.
Locate the subscription or notification option for this discussion (for example, a 'Subscribe to this topic' checkbox) and click it so that you will receive updates for this thread.
Click the 'Post Discussion' or 'Publish' button at the bottom of the form to create the new thread.

tasks.name: Find a popular recent JavaScript array discussion and bookmark it
tasks.description: Search for discussions about JavaScript arrays, filter results to posts within the last 6 months that have at least 10 replies, sort by most upvoted, open the top result, upvote the original post, and bookmark the thread.
steps: From any page on the forum, click inside the search bar located in the header.
Type 'JavaScript arrays' into the search field and press Enter or click the search icon to view results.
On the search results page, open the filter panel and set the date filter to show only discussions from the last 6 months.
In the same filter panel, adjust the replies filter to show only threads with at least 10 replies, then apply the filters.
Locate the sort dropdown on the results page and select the option labeled 'Most Upvoted' or equivalent so that the most upvoted threads appear first.
Click the title of the first discussion in the filtered and sorted results list to open that thread.
On the discussion page, locate the vote control on the original post and click the upvote button to increase its score.
Find the bookmark or 'Save' icon for the thread (often near the title or under the first post) and click it to bookmark this discussion.
Return to the main navigation and click the 'Bookmarks' or 'Saved' section to open your list of bookmarked threads, then click the newly saved JavaScript arrays discussion to reopen it.

tasks.name: Reply to two different users in the same thread and like multiple replies
tasks.description: In a single discussion thread, write replies to posts from two different users, tag one of them using an @mention, and like exactly 3 different replies from three unique users within that thread.
steps: Open the forum homepage and click on the 'Trending' or 'Popular' section to view active discussions.
Select the first discussion that appears in the 'Trending' list by clicking its title to open the thread.
Scroll through the thread until you see a reply from the first user you want to respond to, then click the 'Reply' button directly under that specific reply.
In the reply editor that appears, type a response of at least two sentences addressing that user’s point without using any @mention, then click the 'Post Reply' button.
Scroll further down the thread and find a reply from a different user, then click the 'Reply' button under that second user’s reply.
In the reply editor, start your message by typing '@' followed by the second user’s username from their reply, then complete a response of at least one full sentence and click 'Post Reply'.
Scroll up or down within the same thread and click the 'Like' or thumbs-up icon on three different replies written by three different authors (excluding your own replies).
Once you have liked three distinct replies, click the thread title or breadcrumb link at the top to collapse the view back to the start of the thread.

tasks.name: Follow three high-interest topics with at least 500 followers each
tasks.description: Use the forum’s topic or tag explorer to follow the topics 'UX Design', 'React', and 'Career Advice', choosing versions of these topics that each have at least 500 followers, and confirm you are following exactly three topics when finished.
steps: From the main navigation, click on the 'Topics' or 'Tags' link to open the topic exploration page.
In the topics search field, type 'UX Design' and press Enter to search for related topics.

In the list of UX-related topics, locate a topic named exactly 'UX Design' or the closest match, check its follower count, and if it shows 500 or more followers, click its 'Follow' button; if the first result has fewer than 500 followers, select the next 'UX Design'-related topic with 500 or more followers and click 'Follow' on that one instead.
Clear the topics search field, type 'React', press Enter, and repeat the selection process: choose a 'React' topic that has at least 500 followers and click its 'Follow' button.
Again clear the search field, type 'Career Advice', press Enter, and then choose a 'Career Advice' topic that has 500 or more followers, clicking its 'Follow' button.
After following the three topics, click on the 'Following' or 'My Topics' tab or button within the topics section to view the list of topics you follow.
In the followed topics list, locate and click on the 'UX Design' topic to open its topic page.
Use the breadcrumb or 'Back to My Topics' navigation on the topic page to return to the list of followed topics after viewing the 'UX Design' topic.

tasks.name: Send a private message to a user whose answer has at least 5 upvotes
tasks.description: Find a discussion about portfolio reviews, locate a reply with 5 or more upvotes, open that author’s profile, and send them a private message with a specific subject and body text asking for feedback.
steps: Click on the search bar in the header and type 'portfolio review', then press Enter to view matching discussions.
On the search results page, open the first discussion that mentions portfolios or portfolio reviews by clicking its title.
Scroll through the replies in the discussion until you find a reply that has at least 5 upvotes, indicated by its vote count next to the reply.
Click on the username or avatar of the author of that reply to open their user profile page.
On the user’s profile page, click the 'Message', 'Send Private Message', or similar button to open the private messaging form.
In the subject field of the message, type 'Request for portfolio feedback'.

In the message body, type a short message of 3–5 sentences explaining that you saw their highly upvoted reply in the portfolio discussion and asking if they can provide feedback on improving your portfolio (do not include any external links).
Click the 'Send' button on the messaging form to send the private message to the user.

tasks.name: Update profile with new display name, bio, and timezone preferences
tasks.description: Go to your account settings and change your display name to 'Alex UX Researcher', add a bio of 20–30 words describing your role and interests, set your timezone to GMT+1, and enable daily summary notifications.
steps: From the forum header, click on your avatar or username to open the user menu.
In the user menu, select the 'Settings' or 'Profile Settings' option to open your account settings page.
In the profile section, locate the display name field, delete the current value, and type 'Alex UX Researcher' as the new display name.
Find the bio or 'About Me' text area and replace any existing text with a new bio of 20–30 words describing your work in UX research and your interests in design and community discussions.
Navigate to the 'Account', 'Preferences', or 'Localization' tab within settings and locate the timezone dropdown.
Open the timezone dropdown list and select the option labeled 'GMT+1' or 'UTC+1' from the available timezones.
Scroll to the notifications section, find the option for 'Daily summary' or 'Daily email digest', and switch its toggle or checkbox to the ON position.
Click the 'Save Changes' or 'Update Profile' button at the bottom of the settings page to apply your updated profile information and preferences.

tasks.name: Report a spam reply in a discussion with 'spam' in the title
tasks.description: Search for a discussion with the word 'spam' in its title, find a reply containing a link and 0 upvotes, and submit a spam report for that specific reply using the forum’s reporting tools.
steps: Use the header search bar to type 'spam' and press Enter to show discussions with that keyword.
From the search results, click on the first discussion whose title includes the word 'spam' to open it.
Scroll through the discussion replies and look for a reply that contains at least one hyperlink and a visible vote count of 0 upvotes.
On that reply, click the 'More' menu icon (often shown as three dots) or the report flag icon associated with the reply.
From the menu that appears, select the 'Report', 'Flag', or 'Report Post' option to open the reporting dialog.
In the report reason dropdown or selection list, choose 'Spam' or the closest equivalent spam-related reason.
If a text field for additional details is available, type a short note such as 'Contains unsolicited promotional link' and then click the 'Submit', 'Report', or 'Flag' button to send the report.

tasks.name: Create a product feedback thread with a 3-option poll lasting 7 days
tasks.description: In the 'Product Feedback' category, create a new discussion that includes a poll with exactly 3 answer options, set the poll duration to 7 days, and disable multiple-choice voting so participants can choose only one option.
steps: From the main navigation, click on 'Categories' and then select the 'Product Feedback' category from the categories list.
On the 'Product Feedback' page, click the 'New Discussion' or 'Start Feedback Thread' button.
In the discussion title field, type 'Which feature should we prioritize next?'.
In the body text area, write at least two sentences describing that you are collecting feedback on which feature should be developed next and briefly outline the options.
Within the discussion creation form, click the 'Add Poll', 'Create Poll', or similar button to open the poll configuration section.
In the poll question field, type 'What should we build next?'.
Add three poll options by entering 'Dark mode', 'Mobile app improvements', and 'Advanced search filters' into three separate option fields.
Locate the poll settings area and set the poll duration to end in 7 days by either typing '7' in a duration field or selecting '7 days' from a dropdown.
In the same poll settings, find the multiple-choice option and disable it so that users can select only one poll option.
After confirming the poll question and options are filled in, click the 'Post Discussion' or 'Publish' button to create the thread with the attached poll.
